raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California Film & TV Jobs Act aims to reverse the trend of production flight leading to job losses in California.",
    "The legislation proposes to increase California's tax credit program from $330 million to $750 million annually starting July 1, 2025.",
    "AB 1138 aims to raise the base tax credit rate from 20% to 35%.",
    "The proposed legislation will expand the definition of 'Qualified Motion Picture' while maintaining a $1 million minimum spending requirement per production.",
    "The proposed reforms include eliminating the 50% ownership or 10-year lease requirement for productions utilizing certified sound stages.",
    "California's film and television industry is identified as a vital part of the state's economy.",
    "The Entertainment Union Coalition represents more than 165,000 skilled entertainment industry workers in California.",
    "The Economic impact of the entertainment industry extends beyond Los Angeles to other parts of California.",
    "The proposed tax credits are seen as essential for retaining jobs and supporting small businesses in the entertainment industry.",
    "The modernization of the Film & TV Tax Credit program is intended to make California more competitive with other locations."
  ]
}
```
